jobs.basic_qualifications: - PhD student in Economics (enrolled in 3rd year or more and not currently on the job market)
jobs.description:
We are looking for detail-oriented, organized, and responsible individuals who are eager to apply causal-inference techniques to business-relevant engineering problems.

These are full-time positions at 40 hours per week, with compensation awarded on an hourly basis. You will learn how to apply theoretical econometrics techniques to real-world problems and collaborate with economists, scientists, and product managers. These skills will translate well into writing applied chapters in your dissertation and provide you with work experience that may help with placement.

Key job responsibilities
Well versed with power analysis in experimentation so that they can quickly apply them to our our problem space.

About the team
Central Latency own the latency program for Stores and are responsible for:

Vision: Setting a long-term architecture plan to reduce latency
Goals: establishing Store-wide latency goals
Guardrails: monitoring customer latency with automated tooling
Science: understand the impact of latency

jobs.description:
Amazon Ads is looking for an experienced solution architect to join its global solutions architecture team advising ISVs (AdTech, MartTech and analytics), diverse range of digital advertising agencies as well as large customers who have invested in API-based advertising solutions.
Amazon Ads helps brands create experiences that delight customers and deliver meaningful results. With 300+ million worldwide active customers, and first-party insights based on shopping, streaming and browsing signals, Amazon enables brands to craft innovative campaigns that help customers find and discover anything they want. Our solutions on Amazon.com, services like Twitch, Freevee, Alexa, Amazon Music, and partnerships with third-party publishers and exchanges make Amazon Ads the ultimate amplifier for brands to reach the right audiences in the right places, both on and off Amazon. Amazon Advertising is a key strategic focus for Amazon and is in a rapid and exciting growth phase.
We start with the customer and work backwards in everything we do, including advertising. If you’re interested in joining an innovative team working to build a unique, world-class advertising business with a relentless focus on the customer, you’ve come to the right place.
Amazon Ads Partners help brands achieve and exceed their desired outcomes through tech innovation and expert managed services. Our team builds partnerships across the advertising landscape to drive innovation and growth for all brands, including those that do not sell on Amazon.com. In order to accelerate the next phase of Amazon Ads development, we are committed to transforming the depth, scale and ambition of our partnerships with a diverse landscape of partners across a full range of independent agencies, as well as AdTech and MarTech SaaS providers. With a full suite of ad tech products available, development of partner technical solutions is foundational for customer success.
As Ads partners work across all ad products, supply sources and geographies, this is an exciting opportunity to support the business. As trusted partners and customer advocates, our solutions architects advise organizations on how to develop advanced cloud-based solutions and prioritize their ad tech roadmap. You will have the opportunity to build mindshare and broad use of Amazon ad tech including Amazon Marketing Cloud within the digital advertising community. You should also have a demonstrated ability to provide critical support to address business, products, and technical challenges. You will be partnering with other teams within Ads and AWS such as product, business development, service teams, marketing, and sales, prioritizing your initiatives and aligning goals to maximize long term value.

Key job responsibilities
• Build and maintain relationships with tech leaders across ad agencies, SaaS providers and large customers to promote awareness of Amazon Ads ad tech.
• Build relationships with partners and customers to become a trusted advisor for their most critical workloads/solutions that support digital advertising and marketing transformations. Hands-on solution architect with strong REST API and SDK development skills to build and scale solutions with partners
• Support Global leaders who own achievement of revenue objectives through the adoption of ad products, applications, software, and services, in partnership with business development.
•Serve as key business partner to educate customers on the value proposition of Amazon ad tech and participate in architectural discussions to ensure solutions are designed for successful deployment.
• Build strong relationships with internal product teams to ensure alignment of the product roadmap, vision, and strategy to fully take advantage of future changes and new markets.
• Support the education and training of partners on the positioning of Amazon Ads and contribute to industry thought leadership.

Key Requirements:
Microservices & SOA — designing and building distributed systems
Application Integration — API management, event-driven architectures
Cloud Technologies — AWS, Azure, or similar platforms
Emerging Tech — Gen AI, MCP, and agentic solutions


A day in the life
On a day to day basis you will work with Amazon Ads partners to create awareness for, implement, and activate advertising technology use cases.
Collaborate with key stakeholders internally to ensure we bring back customer feedback and increase the efficiency by improving self service capabilities of advertising products
jobs.description_short: Amazon Ads is looking for an experienced solution architect to join its global solutions architecture team advising ISVs (AdTech, MartTech and analytics), diverse range of digital advertising agencies

jobs.description:
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly workforce and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Key job responsibilities
* Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce
* Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises
* Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives
* Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
* Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
* Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud
* Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
* Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)

Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Area Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.
jobs.description_short: Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager.

jobs.title: Area Manager II

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience utilizing systems and tools involving email, web, analytics, and CRM (Marketo, Tableau, Salesforce, etc.) for targeting, segmentation, reporting for demand generation
- Experience using data and metrics to drive improvements
- Experience building, executing and scaling cross-functional programs or marketing campaigns from concept to completion
- Experience with A/B testing, especially around audience segmentation and targeting
jobs.description:
Amazon is seeking a talented and data-driven Email Lifecycle Campaign Marketing Manager to join the Relay acquisition marketing team. In this role, you will own the end to end development, and execution of email marketing campaigns across the full customer lifecycle — from acquisition and onboarding to engagement, retention, and re-engagement. You will partner cross-functionally with product, data science, and program teams to build highly personalized, segmented campaigns that drive measurable impact at scale. The ideal candidate brings 5+ years of email marketing experience, deep expertise in marketing automation platforms (such as Salesforce Marketing Cloud or Braze), and a strong analytical mindset with a passion for A/B testing and performance optimization. You thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and can translate complex data insights into compelling customer communications that move the needle. A bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field is required, with an MBA preferred. If you are a customer-obsessed marketer who is passionate about building lifecycle programs that delight customers and drive business growth, we want to hear from you.


Key job responsibilities
- Own the full-funnel email calendar — Develop and manage a comprehensive email marketing calendar that spans all lifecycle stages, ensuring timely, relevant, and consistent communication across acquisition, onboarding, engagement, retention, and win-back campaigns.
- Drive measurable revenue and engagement KPIs — Set, track, and report on performance metrics including open rates, click-through rates, conversion rates, and incremental revenue, delivering weekly and monthly insights and optimizations.
- Build and scale personalization and segmentation strategies — Partner with data science and analytics teams to develop advanced audience segmentation models and dynamic content frameworks that deliver highly relevant, 1:1 customer experiences at Amazon’s scale.
- Lead A/B and multivariate testing programs — Design and execute a rigorous testing roadmap across subject lines, send cadences, content, and CTAs to continuously improve campaign performance and establish email best practices across the organization.
- Be the POC for cross-functional campaign execution including working with procurement, product, compliance and learning/ training teams, all of which impact the Amazon Relay Carrier experience.

About the team
This role reports into the Amazon Relay Acquisition team, which is responsible for carrier onboarding and engagement for the vast network of Amazon Relay carriers. Take a look at amazon.relay.com for more information on Amazon Relay!
jobs.description_short: Amazon is seeking a talented and data-driven Email Lifecycle Campaign Marketing Manager to join the Relay acquisition marketing team. In this role, you will own the end to end development, and execution

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree
- Aviation industry background is required, with demonstrable experience in airline operations, cargo airline operations, or air freight management.
- Experience in carrier operations management, airline OCC coordination, flight dispatch, or network operations control.
- Proven ability to manage real-time operational disruptions, coordinate recovery actions, and make time-critical decisions in a dynamic aviation environment.
- Strong understanding of airline operational processes, including flight planning, crew scheduling constraints, aircraft maintenance windows, and airport slot coordination.
- Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret operational performance data, conduct root cause analysis, and translate findings into actionable improvements.
- Proven track record of working in cross-functional, multi-stakeholder environments, navigating ambiguity, and managing multiple priorities simultaneously in a fast-paced, deadline-driven operation.
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, concise operational reports and briefings for both frontline teams and senior leadership.
- Self-starter with strong business judgement and a commitment to operational excellence.
jobs.description:
Do you have a passion for aviation? Are you interested in helping shape the future of air cargo operations in Europe? Amazon Air is seeking a highly motivated aviation professional to join our Air Carrier Operations team as Air Carrier Ops Manager, serving as the primary operational interface between Amazon and its partner airlines across the European middle-mile air transportation network. In this role, you will drive operational excellence, manage carrier relationships, and ensure the safe, efficient, and reliable execution of flight operations. Your work will directly impact the performance of Amazon Air's European network and the experience of our customers. If you are ready to bring your aviation expertise to a fast-paced, innovative environment, then join us in making Amazon history!

The Air Carrier Ops Manager will lead the day-to-day operational engagement with partner carriers, overseeing flight operations performance, network disruption management, charter coordination, ground handling oversight, and carrier expansion programmes. This role requires a strong aviation operations background, the ability to manage complex multi-stakeholder environments, and a hands-on approach to driving continuous improvement across all aspects of carrier operations.


Key job responsibilities
In this role, you will be responsible for:
• Leading regular operational oversight with partner carrier Operations Control Centres (OCC) and internal network control teams, ensuring alignment on daily flight operations, disruption recovery, and operational priorities, with increased cadence during peak periods.
• Monitoring and analysing carrier operational performance metrics, including on-time performance (OTP) reconciliation, spare aircraft utilisation, network flight cancellations, and fleet serviceability.
• Conducting daily network surveillance across the European route network, performing root cause analysis on disruptions and irregularities, and driving corrective actions with carrier partners.
• Managing end-to-end schedule operations via AirVision, including schedule implementation, cancellations, SSIM comparison and reconciliation with carrier systems, and upload to internal planning platforms.
• Coordinating ad-hoc charter operations from feasibility assessment through execution, including loadability evaluation, regulatory compliance, and lessons learnt reporting.
• Overseeing ground handling performance and station-level transitions, including ramp metrics, new station launches, handler changeovers, and infrastructure projects.
• Serving as primary point of contact for BSA operations with airline partners, including delay reconciliation and carrier expansion programmes.
• Developing SOPs, driving continuous improvement across carrier operations, and representing Amazon Air with partner carriers, airport authorities, and ground handling providers.
• Travelling to operational sites across Europe up to 20% of the time.
jobs.description_short: Do you have a passion for aviation? Are you interested in helping shape the future of air cargo operations in Europe? Amazon Air is seeking a highly motivated aviation professional to join our Air Carrier
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Experience with airline schedule management systems such as AirVision (Sabre), AIMS, or equivalent, including SSIM file management and schedule reconciliation processes.
- Experience with carrier on-time performance management, delay attribution, and contractual performance reconciliation.
- Familiarity with cargo aviation operations, including ULD management, dangerous goods regulations (IATA DGR), air waybill processes, and charter operations coordination.
- Experience working directly with airline OCC teams, ground handling agents, and airport slot coordinators in a European aviation environment.
- Knowledge of European aviation regulatory requirements, including EU Slot Regulation, EASA operational standards.
- Proficiency in business intelligence and reporting tools such as AWS QuickSight, Tableau, or similar platforms, including SQL skills for querying and analysing complex aviation network data.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of program or project management experience
- 3+ years of defining and implementing process improvement initiatives using data and metrics experience
- Knowledge of Excel (Pivot Tables, VLookUps) at an advanced level and SQL
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements

jobs.description:
Amazon One Medical (AOM) is passionately working to build a best-in-class healthcare product designed to make high-quality healthcare easy to access. We own Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) within AOM, which represents the comprehensive financial lifecycle that manages patient accounts from their initial healthcare service request/appointment booking through to the final payment settlement. This process is fundamental to AOM's financial stability and operational efficiency, allowing us to focus primarily on delivering quality patient care.

We are seeking a talented Program Manager who is familiar with the billing complexities of the healthcare industry. You will have strong math or analytical skills to ensure we are accurately recording cash payments into our billing system, have strong communication skills to be able to explain our cash reconciliations to external stakeholders, and identify solutions that can automate how we record our cash. This role will partner closely with RCM Operations, Healthcare Finance Operations, Accounting, Finance, and our Health System Partners (external to Amazon).

Key job responsibilities
- Monitor and reconcile cash payments across all payment channels (insurance, patient, HSP) to ensure accurate recording in the billing system
- Develop and maintain cash posting controllership and workflows to minimize posting errors and improve automation opportunities
- Track key metrics including cash posting accuracy, posting lag time, and reconciliation variances while creating dashboards for performance visibility
- Partner with RCM Operations, Healthcare Finance Operations, Accounting, Finance, and Health System Partners to resolve payment discrepancies and streamline cash workflows
- Lead cash reconciliation processes and communicate findings to external stakeholders including health system partners and payers
- Conduct root cause analysis on payment posting errors and identify solutions to automate cash recording processes
- Ensure cash posting processes comply with healthcare regulations, accounting standards, and support month-end close and audit requirements

About the team
Our team puts a high value on work-life balance. It isn’t about how many hours you spend at home or at work; it’s about the flow you establish that brings energy to both parts of your life. We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

jobs.title: Cash Program Manager, Amazon One Medical Revenue Cycle Management

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of customer service in a healthcare call center environment experience
- 1+ years of medical billing, insurance claims, or healthcare revenue cycle experience
- Experience in Microsoft Office Suite using electronic health records (EHR) or customer relationship management (CRM) systems
jobs.description:
Application deadline: Apr 21, 2026

As we continue to expand and transform the primary care experience, we’re looking for passionate people to help manage our patient inquiries, using CI CARE framework that makes One Medical unique. As a Centralized Support Specialist II Billing Specialist, you’ll execute our mission of delivering high-quality care and service. Specifically, you’ll take patient phone calls and handle patient conversations to deliver world class care.

You are a strong and innovative problem-solver, who is driven to help people. You have mastered the art of customer-service, administrative work and are motivated to cultivate change in healthcare. You are currently looking for your next opportunity at an organization that is transforming healthcare we would love to connect.

Key job responsibilities
Handle patient phone calls and message inquiries, de-escalate issues, ensure patient satisfaction, and assist with navigating healthcare needs including insurance, billing, medical records, authorizations, and referrals, using impeccable C-I-CARE (a framework containing the key elements of a great interaction and effective communication that we use with patients and each other) in all interactions to ensure a positive experience.

Collaborate with providers and other operations team members to complete urgent tasks pertaining to patient billing issues and questions.
Proactively reach out to patients with open balances to assist with payment options, educate admins and patients on health insurance benefits, answer complex billing inquiries, resolve payment processing issues, support One Medical’s efforts to empower patients in resolving financial responsibilities, and maintain service level agreements in response time to admins and patients.
Assisting admins with our member’s billing needs via 1Life’s tasking system to provide the best customer service.
Investigating claims through insurance to ensure they were processed according to the patient benefit plan, including Reviewing and reconciling patient and insurance balances for accuracy.
Master our technology suite including but not limited to RingCentral, Slack, G-suite, Zoom, and our Electronic Medical Record System 1Life, to be able to interact with team members and patients and complete daily work.
Contribute to team development through rounding, attending team huddles, participating in team problem solving, supporting all in-office providers with urgent & stat patient needs.

Key job responsibilities
A phone customer service healthcare associate for patient billing inquiries at One Medical plays a crucial role in ensuring patient satisfaction and maintaining clear communication regarding financial matters. Key responsibilities include answering incoming calls professionally and efficiently, addressing patients' billing questions and concerns with empathy and accuracy. The associate must have a thorough understanding of medical billing practices, insurance policies, and One Medical's specific billing procedures to provide detailed explanations of charges, co-pays, and deductibles. They are expected to research and resolve billing discrepancies, process payments, and assist with setting up payment plans when necessary. The role requires strong problem-solving skills to navigate complex billing situations and the ability to clearly communicate financial information to patients and deescalate when necessary. Additionally, the associate must maintain patient confidentiality, adhere to HIPAA regulations, and accurately document all interactions in the company's customer relationship management system. They should also be able to escalate complex issues to appropriate departments when needed and follow up with patients to ensure resolution. Excellent customer service skills, attention to detail, and the ability to remain calm under pressure are essential for success in this position.

A day in the life
In this role, you'll handle inbound calls from One Medical patients (customers) regarding their medical bills, insurance claims, and payment inquiries. Daily activities include reviewing patient accounts, processing payments, explaining EOBs, and coordinating with internal stakeholders such as medical billing specialists, insurance verification teams, and healthcare providers to resolve billing discrepancies. You'll solve problems like incorrect charges, insurance claim rejections, payment plan requests, and billing confusion. Regular communication with insurance companies to verify benefits and claim status is essential. The role requires documenting all interactions in the electronic health record system and following up on pending issues.

About the team
Amazon One Medical's call center team combines healthcare expertise with Amazon's customer-obsessed culture to deliver exceptional patient support. Our team has focused on making healthcare more accessible and less complicated for members. We handle billing inquiries with empathy and efficiency. Our culture emphasizes leadership principles of ownership and continuous improvement, supported by ongoing training and development. Team members work in a diverse, inclusive environment where success is measured by positive patient impact while maintaining the highest standards of healthcare privacy and professionalism.

jobs.title: Customer Service Specialist - Healthcare Billing, Revenue Cycle Management, Amazon One Medical

jobs.basic_qualifications:

- Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or 2+ years of Amazon (blue badge/FTE) experience

jobs.description:
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly workforce and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Key job responsibilities
* Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce
* Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises
* Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives
* Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
* Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
* Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud
* Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
* Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)

Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Area Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.
jobs.description_short: Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager.
